revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]][[天文博士]]へのリダイレクト
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[天文博士]]

'''天文博士'''（てんもんはかせ）とは、[[律令制]]の[[陰陽寮]]に設置された教官で[[天文道]]のことを担当する。[[正七位下]]相当。定員は1名であるが、後に[[権官]]も設けられた。
== 概要 == [[天体]]を観測して[[天文学|天文]]のことに異変（[[月食]]や[[彗星]]などの[[天文現象]]）が発見されれば、天文書に基づいて吉凶を占ってその内容を密封した上で天皇に奏聞する[[天文密奏]]を行うことと、天文生（定員10名）に天文のことを教授することを掌った。なお、天文書は天文生が読むことは禁じられていたことから（[[雑令]]秘書玄象条）、天文博士など限られた人しか読むことができなかった。
平安時代中期に[[賀茂保憲]]から天文道を教授された[[安倍晴明]]が天文博士に任ぜられて以後、[[阿倍氏|安倍氏]]（[[土御門家]]）による世襲の地位となった。
== 参考文献 == *佐藤均「天文博士」（『国史大辞典 9』（吉川弘文館、1988年） ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8） *佐藤均「天文博士」（『平安時代史事典』（角川書店、1994年） ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7）
{{DEFAULTSORT:てんもんはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:日本の天文学史]] [[Category:陰陽寮]]

revision.comment: 水野白楓 がページ「[[権天文博士]]」を「[[天文博士]]」に移動しました

{{脚注の不足|date=2019年10月}} '''天文博士'''（てんもんはかせ）とは、[[律令制]]の[[陰陽寮]]に設置された教官で[[天文道]]のことを担当する。[[正七位|正七位下]]相当。定員は1名であるが、後に[[権官]]も設けられた。
== 概要 == [[天体]]を観測して[[天文道|天文]]のことに異変（[[月食]]や[[彗星]]などの[[天文現象]]）が発見されれば、天文書に基づいて吉凶を占ってその内容を密封した上で天皇に奏聞する[[天文密奏]]を行うことと、天文生（定員10名）に天文のことを教授することを掌った。なお、天文書は天文生が読むことは禁じられていたことから（[[雑令]]秘書玄象条）、天文博士など限られた人しか読むことができなかった。
[[平安時代]]中期に[[賀茂保憲]]から天文道を教授された[[安倍晴明]]が天文博士に任ぜられて以後、[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]]（[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]）による世襲の地位となった。
== 参考文献 == *佐藤均「天文博士」（『国史大辞典 9』（吉川弘文館、1988年） ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8） *佐藤均「天文博士」（『平安時代史事典』（角川書店、1994年） ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7）

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later~~~~}} [[{{PAGENAME}}]] is a topic related to [[Onmyodo]] ==Onmyoji== {{Short description|Japanese court diviner position}} {{Use dmy dates|date=February 2021}} {{Other uses|Onmyoji (disambiguation)}} {{Multiple issues| {{more footnotes|date=January 2...'

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}} {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
==In media== === Books === * ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Aramata]], published in 1985 * ''[[Onmyōji (novel series)|Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Baku Yumemakura]], published in 1988 * ''[[The Summer of the Ubume]]'', ''[[Mōryō no Hako]]'' and other Kyōgokudō series, written by [[Natsuhiko Kyogoku]], published in 1994 * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Miyako|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'', written by [[Soichiro Watase]], published in 2001 * ''[[Shōnen Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Mitsuru Yūki]], published in 2001 * ''[[Rental Magica]]'', written by {{ill|Makoto Sanda|ja|三田誠}}, published in 2004 * ''{{ill|Bakemonogatari|ja|化物語}}'', written by [[Nisio Isin]], published in 2005 * ''[[Fairy Navigator Runa]]'', written by {{ill|Miyoko Ikeda|ja|池田美代子}}, published in 2008 * ''[[Tokyo Ravens]]'', written by {{ill|Kōhei Azano|ja|あざの耕平}}, published in 2010
=== Manga and anime ===
* ''[[Tokyo Babylon]]'', written by [[Clamp (manga artists)|Clamp]], published in 1990 * ''[[X (manga)|X]]'', written by Clamp, published in 1992 * ''[[Shaman King]]'', written by [[Hiroyuki Takei]], published in 1998 * ''[[Gag Manga Biyori]]'', written by [[Kōsuke Masuda]], published in 2000 * ''[[Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi]]'', created by [[Gainax]], published in 2001 * ''[[Amatsuki]]'', written by {{ill|Shinobu Takayama|ja|高山しのぶ}}, published in 2002 * ''[[Gin Tama]]'', written by [[Hideaki Sorachi]], published in 2003 * ''[[xxxHolic]]'', written by Clamp, published in 2003 * ''[[Onmyō Taisenki]]'', written by Yoshihiko Tomizawa, published in 2003 * ''[[Musashi Gundoh]]'', based on an unused story by [[Monkey Punch]], published in 2006 * ''{{ill|Shibariya Komachi|ja|縛り屋小町}}'', written by [[Mick Takeuchi]], published in 2006 * ''[[Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Shiibashi]], published in 2008 * ''[[Twin Star Exorcists]]'', written by {{ill|Yoshiaki Sukeno|ja|助野嘉昭}}, published in 2013 * ''[[The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World |The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World]]'', written by Kiichi Kosuzu, published in 2018 * ''[[MAO (manga)|MAO]]'', written by [[Rumiko Takahashi]], published in 2019
=== Films === *''[[Tokyo: The Last Megalopolis]]'', a 1988 Japanese fantasy film directed by [[Akio Jissōji]], based on the novel ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'' by Hiroshi Aramata *[[Onmyōji (film)|''Onmyōji'' (film)]], a 2001 Japanese film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura and released in the U.S. in 2003 as ''Onmyōji - The Yin Yang Master'' **''[[Onmyōji 2]]'', the 2003 sequel *''[[The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity]]'', a 2020 Chinese fantasy film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura directed by [[Guo Jingming]], and starring [[Mark Chao]] and [[Deng Lun]] *''[[The Yinyang Master]]'', a 2021 Chinese fantasy film directed by Li Weiran, and starring [[Chen Kun]], [[Zhou Xun]] and [[William Chan]] based on the game that was based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura
=== Video games === * ''{{ill|Kuon no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'', developed by [[FOG Inc.]], released in 1998 * ''[[Tokyo Majin Gakuen]]'', produced by {{ill|Shūhō Imai|ja|今井秋芳}}, released in 1998 * ''[[Kuon]]'', developed by [[FromSoftware]], released in 2004 * ''[[11eyes: Tsumi to Batsu to Aganai no Shōjo]]'', developed by {{ill|Lass (video game developer)|lt=Lass|ja|Lass}}, released in 2008 * ''[[Naraka: Bladepoint]]'', developed by [[24 Entertainment]], released in 2021 features the character [[Kurumi Tsuchimikado]], who according to game lore is the first female Onmyōji, descended from a long line of Onmyōdō masters.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.narakathegame.com/index.html|title = Naraka: Bladepoint - New Hero Out Now!}}</ref>
== See also ==
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

revision.text:
{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
==In media== === Books === * ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Aramata]], published in 1985 * ''[[Onmyōji (novel series)|Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Baku Yumemakura]], published in 1988 * ''[[The Summer of the Ubume]]'', ''[[Mōryō no Hako]]'' and other Kyōgokudō series, written by [[Natsuhiko Kyogoku]], published in 1994 * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Miyako|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'', written by [[Soichiro Watase]], published in 2001 * ''[[Shōnen Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Mitsuru Yūki]], published in 2001 * ''[[Rental Magica]]'', written by {{ill|Makoto Sanda|ja|三田誠}}, published in 2004 * ''{{ill|Bakemonogatari|ja|化物語}}'', written by [[Nisio Isin]], published in 2005 * ''[[Fairy Navigator Runa]]'', written by {{ill|Miyoko Ikeda|ja|池田美代子}}, published in 2008 * ''[[Tokyo Ravens]]'', written by {{ill|Kōhei Azano|ja|あざの耕平}}, published in 2010
=== Manga and anime ===
* ''[[Tokyo Babylon]]'', written by [[Clamp (manga artists)|Clamp]], published in 1990 * ''[[X (manga)|X]]'', written by Clamp, published in 1992 * ''[[Shaman King]]'', written by [[Hiroyuki Takei]], published in 1998 * ''[[Gag Manga Biyori]]'', written by [[Kōsuke Masuda]], published in 2000 * ''[[Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi]]'', created by [[Gainax]], published in 2001 * ''[[Amatsuki]]'', written by {{ill|Shinobu Takayama|ja|高山しのぶ}}, published in 2002 * ''[[Gin Tama]]'', written by [[Hideaki Sorachi]], published in 2003 * ''[[xxxHolic]]'', written by Clamp, published in 2003 * ''[[Onmyō Taisenki]]'', written by Yoshihiko Tomizawa, published in 2003 * ''[[Musashi Gundoh]]'', based on an unused story by [[Monkey Punch]], published in 2006 * ''{{ill|Shibariya Komachi|ja|縛り屋小町}}'', written by [[Mick Takeuchi]], published in 2006 * ''[[Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Shiibashi]], published in 2008 * ''[[Twin Star Exorcists]]'', written by {{ill|Yoshiaki Sukeno|ja|助野嘉昭}}, published in 2013 * ''[[The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World]]'', written by Kiichi Kosuzu, published in 2018 * ''[[MAO (manga)|MAO]]'', written by [[Rumiko Takahashi]], published in 2019
=== Films === *''[[Tokyo: The Last Megalopolis]]'', a 1988 Japanese fantasy film directed by [[Akio Jissōji]], based on the novel ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'' by Hiroshi Aramata *[[Onmyōji (film)|''Onmyōji'' (film)]], a 2001 Japanese film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura and released in the U.S. in 2003 as ''Onmyōji - The Yin Yang Master'' **''[[Onmyōji 2]]'', the 2003 sequel *''[[The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity]]'', a 2020 Chinese fantasy film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura directed by [[Guo Jingming]], and starring [[Mark Chao]] and [[Deng Lun]] *''[[The Yinyang Master]]'', a 2021 Chinese fantasy film directed by Li Weiran, and starring [[Chen Kun]], [[Zhou Xun]] and [[William Chan]] based on the game that was based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura
=== Video games === * ''{{ill|Kuon no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'', developed by [[FOG Inc.]], released in 1998 * ''[[Tokyo Majin Gakuen]]'', produced by {{ill|Shūhō Imai|ja|今井秋芳}}, released in 1998 * ''[[Kuon]]'', developed by [[FromSoftware]], released in 2004 * ''[[11eyes: Tsumi to Batsu to Aganai no Shōjo]]'', developed by {{ill|Lass (video game developer)|lt=Lass|ja|Lass}}, released in 2008 * ''[[Naraka: Bladepoint]]'', developed by [[24 Entertainment]], released in 2021 features the character [[Kurumi Tsuchimikado]], who according to game lore is the first female Onmyōji, descended from a long line of Onmyōdō masters.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.narakathegame.com/index.html|title = Naraka: Bladepoint - New Hero Out Now!}}</ref>
== See also ==
* [[Abe no Seimei]] * [[Da Liu Ren]] * [[Goryō]] * [[Itako]] * [[Kuji-in]] * [[Onmyōdō]] * [[Sanpaku]] * [[Seimei Shrine]] * [[Senji Ryakketsu]] * [[Shikigami]] * [[Shinigami]] * [[Tengenjutsu (fortune telling)]] * [[Ushi no toki mairi]]

{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
==In media== === Books === * ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Aramata]], published in 1985 * ''[[Onmyōji (novel series)|Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Baku Yumemakura]], published in 1988 * ''[[The Summer of the Ubume]]'', ''[[Mōryō no Hako]]'' and other Kyōgokudō series, written by [[Natsuhiko Kyogoku]], published in 1994 * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Miyako|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'', written by [[Soichiro Watase]], published in 2001 * ''[[Shōnen Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Mitsuru Yūki]], published in 2001 * ''[[Rental Magica]]'', written by {{ill|Makoto Sanda|ja|三田誠}}, published in 2004 * ''{{ill|Bakemonogatari|ja|化物語}}'', written by [[Nisio Isin]], published in 2005 * ''[[Fairy Navigator Runa]]'', written by {{ill|Miyoko Ikeda|ja|池田美代子}}, published in 2008 * ''[[Tokyo Ravens]]'', written by {{ill|Kōhei Azano|ja|あざの耕平}}, published in 2010
=== Manga and anime ===
* ''[[Tokyo Babylon]]'', written by [[Clamp (manga artists)|Clamp]], published in 1990 * ''[[X (manga)|X]]'', written by Clamp, published in 1992 * ''[[Shaman King]]'', written by [[Hiroyuki Takei]], published in 1998 * ''[[Gag Manga Biyori]]'', written by [[Kōsuke Masuda]], published in 2000 * ''[[Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi]]'', created by [[Gainax]], published in 2001 * ''[[Amatsuki]]'', written by {{ill|Shinobu Takayama|ja|高山しのぶ}}, published in 2002 * ''[[Gin Tama]]'', written by [[Hideaki Sorachi]], published in 2003 * ''[[xxxHolic]]'', written by Clamp, published in 2003 * ''[[Onmyō Taisenki]]'', written by Yoshihiko Tomizawa, published in 2003 * ''[[Musashi Gundoh]]'', based on an unused story by [[Monkey Punch]], published in 2006 * ''{{ill|Shibariya Komachi|ja|縛り屋小町}}'', written by [[Mick Takeuchi]], published in 2006 * ''[[Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Shiibashi]], published in 2008 * ''[[Twin Star Exorcists]]'', written by {{ill|Yoshiaki Sukeno|ja|助野嘉昭}}, published in 2013 * ''[[The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World]]'', written by Kiichi Kosuzu, published in 2018 * ''[[MAO (manga)|MAO]]'', written by [[Rumiko Takahashi]], published in 2019
=== Films === *''[[Tokyo: The Last Megalopolis]]'', a 1988 Japanese fantasy film directed by [[Akio Jissōji]], based on the novel ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'' by Hiroshi Aramata *[[Onmyōji (film)|''Onmyōji'' (film)]], a 2001 Japanese film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura and released in the U.S. in 2003 as ''Onmyōji - The Yin Yang Master'' **''[[Onmyōji 2]]'', the 2003 sequel *''[[The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity]]'', a 2020 Chinese fantasy film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura directed by [[Guo Jingming]], and starring [[Mark Chao]] and [[Deng Lun]] *''[[The Yinyang Master]]'', a 2021 Chinese fantasy film directed by Li Weiran, and starring [[Chen Kun]], [[Zhou Xun]] and [[William Chan]] based on the game that was based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura
=== Video games === * ''{{ill|Kuon no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'', developed by [[FOG Inc.]], released in 1998 * ''[[Tokyo Majin Gakuen]]'', produced by {{ill|Shūhō Imai|ja|今井秋芳}}, released in 1998 * ''[[Kuon]]'', developed by [[FromSoftware]], released in 2004 * ''[[11eyes: Tsumi to Batsu to Aganai no Shōjo]]'', developed by {{ill|Lass (video game developer)|lt=Lass|ja|Lass}}, released in 2008 * ''[[Naraka: Bladepoint]]'', developed by [[24 Entertainment]], released in 2021 features the character [[Kurumi Tsuchimikado]], who according to game lore is the first female Onmyōji, descended from a long line of Onmyōdō masters.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.narakathegame.com/index.html|title = Naraka: Bladepoint - New Hero Out Now!}}</ref>

{{draft}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
==In media== === Books === * ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Aramata]], published in 1985 * ''[[Onmyōji (novel series)|Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Baku Yumemakura]], published in 1988 * ''[[The Summer of the Ubume]]'', ''[[Mōryō no Hako]]'' and other Kyōgokudō series, written by [[Natsuhiko Kyogoku]], published in 1994 * ''{{ill|Onmyō no Miyako|ja|陰陽ノ京}}'', written by [[Soichiro Watase]], published in 2001 * ''[[Shōnen Onmyōji]]'', written by [[Mitsuru Yūki]], published in 2001 * ''[[Rental Magica]]'', written by {{ill|Makoto Sanda|ja|三田誠}}, published in 2004 * ''{{ill|Bakemonogatari|ja|化物語}}'', written by [[Nisio Isin]], published in 2005 * ''[[Fairy Navigator Runa]]'', written by {{ill|Miyoko Ikeda|ja|池田美代子}}, published in 2008 * ''[[Tokyo Ravens]]'', written by {{ill|Kōhei Azano|ja|あざの耕平}}, published in 2010
=== Manga and anime ===
* ''[[Tokyo Babylon]]'', written by [[Clamp (manga artists)|Clamp]], published in 1990 * ''[[X (manga)|X]]'', written by Clamp, published in 1992 * ''[[Shaman King]]'', written by [[Hiroyuki Takei]], published in 1998 * ''[[Gag Manga Biyori]]'', written by [[Kōsuke Masuda]], published in 2000 * ''[[Magical Shopping Arcade Abenobashi]]'', created by [[Gainax]], published in 2001 * ''[[Amatsuki]]'', written by {{ill|Shinobu Takayama|ja|高山しのぶ}}, published in 2002 * ''[[Gin Tama]]'', written by [[Hideaki Sorachi]], published in 2003 * ''[[xxxHolic]]'', written by Clamp, published in 2003 * ''[[Onmyō Taisenki]]'', written by Yoshihiko Tomizawa, published in 2003 * ''[[Musashi Gundoh]]'', based on an unused story by [[Monkey Punch]], published in 2006 * ''{{ill|Shibariya Komachi|ja|縛り屋小町}}'', written by [[Mick Takeuchi]], published in 2006 * ''[[Nura: Rise of the Yokai Clan]]'', written by [[Hiroshi Shiibashi]], published in 2008 * ''[[Twin Star Exorcists]]'', written by {{ill|Yoshiaki Sukeno|ja|助野嘉昭}}, published in 2013 * ''[[The Reincarnation of the Strongest Exorcist in Another World]]'', written by Kiichi Kosuzu, published in 2018 * ''[[MAO (manga)|MAO]]'', written by [[Rumiko Takahashi]], published in 2019
=== Films === *''[[Tokyo: The Last Megalopolis]]'', a 1988 Japanese fantasy film directed by [[Akio Jissōji]], based on the novel ''[[Teito Monogatari]]'' by Hiroshi Aramata *[[Onmyōji (film)|''Onmyōji'' (film)]], a 2001 Japanese film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura and released in the U.S. in 2003 as ''Onmyōji - The Yin Yang Master'' **''[[Onmyōji 2]]'', the 2003 sequel *''[[The Yin-Yang Master: Dream of Eternity]]'', a 2020 Chinese fantasy film based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura directed by [[Guo Jingming]], and starring [[Mark Chao]] and [[Deng Lun]] *''[[The Yinyang Master]]'', a 2021 Chinese fantasy film directed by Li Weiran, and starring [[Chen Kun]], [[Zhou Xun]] and [[William Chan]] based on the game that was based on the [[Onmyōji (novel series)|''Onmyōji'' (novel series)]], by Baku Yumemakura
=== Video games === * ''{{ill|Kuon no Kizuna|ja|久遠の絆}}'', developed by [[FOG Inc.]], released in 1998 * ''[[Tokyo Majin Gakuen]]'', produced by {{ill|Shūhō Imai|ja|今井秋芳}}, released in 1998 * ''[[Kuon]]'', developed by [[FromSoftware]], released in 2004 * ''[[11eyes: Tsumi to Batsu to Aganai no Shōjo]]'', developed by {{ill|Lass (video game developer)|lt=Lass|ja|Lass}}, released in 2008 * ''[[Naraka: Bladepoint]]'', developed by [[24 Entertainment]], released in 2021 features the character [[Kurumi Tsuchimikado]], who according to game lore is the first female Onmyōji, descended from a long line of Onmyōdō masters.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.narakathegame.com/index.html|title = Naraka: Bladepoint - New Hero Out Now!}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Sources insufficient|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' (天文博士) was an instructor position established within the [[Ritsuryō system]]'s [[Onmyōryō]], responsible for matters related to [[Tenmon-dō]] (astronomy). It corresponded to the rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}. The position was originally limited to one individual but later included a secondary official ({{ill|Kengyō|ja|権官}}).
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase was tasked with observing celestial bodies and identifying anomalies in astronomical phenomena (e.g., [[lunar eclipses]] or [[comets]]). When such anomalies were discovered, they would interpret the events based on astronomical texts, determine auspicious or ominous meanings, and submit their findings to the emperor through a sealed report, a practice known as {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}}. Additionally, they were responsible for teaching astronomy to Tenmon Students ({{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}}), who had a fixed number of 10. Notably, Tenmon Students were prohibited from reading astronomical texts themselves (as stipulated by the {{ill|Miscellaneous Provisions|ja|雑令}} on the secrecy of documents). Thus, only a select few, including the Tenmon Hakase, had access to these texts.
From the mid-[[Heian period]], following the appointment of {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} as Tenmon Hakase, who had studied Tenmon-dō under {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, the position became hereditary within the {{ill|Abe clan|ja|阿倍氏}} ({{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Kokushi Daijiten* Vol. 9 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8 * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidaishi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:Ritsuryō system in Japan]] [[Category:History of astronomy in Japan]] [[Category:Onmyōryō]] [[ja:天文博士]]

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
