[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). {{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. {{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. {{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
