{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|陰陽博士" to "|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase"
revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Tenmon Hakase|Tenmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Temmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Temmon Hakase|Temmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=Page originally made on english wikipedia in draftspace. g13 deleted and archived on everybodywiki. Go to [[:en:Wikipedia:Requests for undeletion]] to request undeletion of original page [[:en:draft:Temmon Hakase]][[en:draft:Tenmon Hakase]] and import the revisions to here for attribution. View on everybodywiki here [[:every:Temmon Hakase|Temmon Hakase]]. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:01, 16 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=This page was originally created by Immanuelle as a wikipedia draft. It was then automatically transferred to everybodywiki and recovered from there. In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AFC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}[[ja:天文博士]] {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__ [[Onmyodo]]
Later, [[Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].
{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}} '''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官}}) was also created.
== Overview == The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., {{ill|lunar eclipses|ja|月食}}, {{ill|comets|ja|彗星}}, and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.
From the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, following instruction from {{ill|Kamo no Yasunori|ja|賀茂保憲}}, {{ill|Abe no Seimei|ja|安倍晴明}} was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家}}).
== References == * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8. * Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{脚注の不足|date=2019年10月}} '''天文博士'''（てんもんはかせ）とは、[[律令制]]の[[陰陽寮]]に設置された教官で[[天文道]]のことを担当する。[[正七位|正七位下]]相当。定員は1名であるが、後に[[権官]]も設けられた。
== 概要 == [[天体]]を観測して[[天文道|天文]]のことに異変（[[月食]]や[[彗星]]などの[[天文現象]]）が発見されれば、天文書に基づいて吉凶を占ってその内容を密封した上で天皇に奏聞する[[天文密奏]]を行うことと、天文生（定員10名）に天文のことを教授することを掌った。なお、天文書は天文生が読むことは禁じられていたことから（[[雑令]]秘書玄象条）、天文博士など限られた人しか読むことができなかった。
[[平安時代]]中期に[[賀茂保憲]]から天文道を教授された[[安倍晴明]]が天文博士に任ぜられて以後、[[阿倍氏#陰陽道安倍氏|安倍氏]]（[[土御門家#土御門家（安倍氏）|土御門家]]）による世襲の地位となった。
== 参考文献 == *佐藤均「天文博士」（『国史大辞典 9』（吉川弘文館、1988年） ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8） *佐藤均「天文博士」（『平安時代史事典』（角川書店、1994年） ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7）
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:てんもんはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:日本の天文学史]] [[Category:陰陽寮]]

In the future it will be good to import the revisions. Check page history in case this page was moved, since the everybodywiki page may have been under a different title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:10, 11 February 2025 (UTC)}}
{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|qq=|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

== References ==
* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8.
* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

* {{cite book |editor-last=Murakami |editor-first=Shūichi |year=2017 |title=陰陽道叢書 1 古代 |trans-title=Onmyōdō Series 1: The Ancient Times |url= |language=Japanese |location= |publisher=Hanawa Shobō |isbn=978-4626017970}}

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}

Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|qq=|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).
