{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). == Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

== References ==
* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 9* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1988) ISBN 978-4-642-00509-8.
* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

{{AfC comment|1=Note: This draft started from a complete copy of [[Onmyōji]] as it has a lot of redlinks with claims I want to keep. I will trim down into just the claim on an individual thing later[[User:Immanuelle|'''Immanuel'''le]] ❤️💚💙 [[User_talk:Immanuelle|(talk to the cutest Wikipedian)]] 01:41, 25 April 2023 (UTC)}}

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).

== References ==

* Hitoshi Satō, "Tenmon Hakase," in *Heian Jidai-shi Jiten* (Kadokawa Shoten, 1994) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7.

{{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}} is a topic related to __NOINDEX__  [[:en:Onmyodo|Onmyodo]]

Later, [[:en:Emperor Tenmu|Emperor Tenmu]] was such a master of astrology and fugue that he took his own divination tools and told fortunes during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. He also had a deep knowledge of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy, which led to the establishment of the Bureau of Onmyō and Japan's first [[:en:Observatory|Observatory]] in 676, and the use of the term "onmyōji" in 685, which further increased the popularity of the yin-and-yang five phases philosophy. In 718, the [[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]] established the Bureau of Onmyō as an internal department of the Ministry of Center, and when it was stipulated that the positions of {{ill|Temmon Hakase|lt=Doctor of Astrology|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}, {{ill|Onmyō Hakase|lt=Doctor of Onmyō|ja|陰陽博士|1=Onmyō Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyō Hakase|qq=}}, onmyōji, {{ill|Reki Hakase|lt=Doctor of Calendar|ja|暦博士|1=Reki Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Reki Hakase|qq=}}, and {{ill|Rōkoku Hakase|lt=Doctor of Water Clock|ja|漏刻博士|1=Rōkoku Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Rōkoku Hakase|qq=}} should be permanently assigned as technical officers, the Bureau of Onmyō became officially in charge of divination along with the [[:en:Turtle shell|Turtle shell]] diviners who belonged to the [[Department of Divinities]].

{{Cite shortage|date=October 2019}}
'''Tenmon Hakase''' ({{ill|Tenmon Hakase|lt=天文博士|ja|天文博士|1=Tenmon Hakase|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Hakase|qq=}}) was a teaching position established in the {{ill|Onmyōryō|ja|陰陽寮|1=Yin-Yang Bureau|2=ja|3=陰陽寮|4=fr|5=Bureau d'Onmyō|6=zh|7=阴阳寮|8=ko|9=음양료|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onmyōryō|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Bureau|qq=}} under Japan's [[Ritsuryō system]], responsible for matters of {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}} (astronomy). The position was equivalent to {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正七位下|1=Senior Seventh Rank|lt=Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正七位}}, with one appointee, though later an assistant position ({{ill|Gonkan|ja|権官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkan|qq=}}) was also created.

== Overview ==
The Tenmon Hakase's duties included observing celestial phenomena (e.g., [[:en:Lunar eclipse|lunar eclipses]], [[:en:Comet|comets]], and other {{ill|astronomical events|ja|天文現象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical events|qq=}}). If an anomaly was detected, the Tenmon Hakase would consult astronomical texts, interpret omens, and present a sealed report to the emperor in the {{ill|Tenmon Missō|ja|天文密奏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmon Missō|qq=}} ritual. The role also entailed instructing ten {{ill|Tenmonsei|ja|天文生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmonsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} students in astronomy. Notably, the Tenmonsei were forbidden to access astronomical texts, as specified under the {{ill|Miscellaneous Orders|ja|雑令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miscellaneous Orders|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s section on the prohibition of secret texts ({{ill|Hisogenzō|ja|秘書玄象条|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisogenzō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Consequently, only the Tenmon Hakase and a few other authorized individuals could read these texts.

From the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], following instruction from [[:en:Kamo no Yasunori|Kamo no Yasunori]], [[:en:Abe no Seimei|Abe no Seimei]] was appointed Tenmon Hakase. Thereafter, the position became hereditary, controlled by the {{ill|Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|ja|阿倍氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe clan (Onmyōdō)|1=Abe clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657247}} (specifically the {{ill|Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado clan|lt=Tsuchimikado family|ja|土御門家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado family|qq=}}).
